attractions.name: University of Arizona Biosphere 2
attractions.description: Biosphere 2 is a massive Earth system science research facility and museum designed to replicate Earth’s complex ecosystems within a 3.14-acre glass structure. Originally built in the 1980s for closed-system experiments, it now serves as a laboratory for the University of Arizona to study climate change, water cycles, and sustainability (University of Arizona, Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM daily (Last entry 3:30 PM) (Biosphere 2)
attractions.admission: Adults: $27.00 online ($29.00 onsite); Seniors (62+): $25.00 online ($27.00 onsite); Military: $25.00 online ($27.00 onsite); Students (5-17): $15.00; Children (0-4): Free; University of Arizona Students/Faculty: $20.00 (Biosphere 2)
highlights: Five distinct biomes under glass, including a tropical rainforest, a million-gallon ocean, and a fog desert (Biosphere 2).
The Landscape Evolution Observatory (LEO), the world’s largest indoor Earth science experiment (Biosphere 2).
Two massive 'lungs' (geodesic domes) that regulate air pressure within the airtight structure (Wikipedia).
Historical living quarters and laboratories where the original Biospherians resided during two-year missions (University of Arizona News).
Interactive self-guided audio tour experience delivered through the Biosphere 2 mobile app (Biosphere 2).
attractions.tips: Download the Biosphere 2 Experience app before arriving for the audio tour. Wear comfortable walking shoes for the 1-mile path, which includes stairs. Expect high humidity and temperature variations inside the different biomes. Buy tickets online in advance to save $2 per adult, senior, and military ticket (Biosphere 2, Reddit).
attractions.nearby: Oracle State Park, Arizona Zipline Adventures, Catalina State Park, Acadia Ranch Museum (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Unscrewed Theater
attractions.description: Unscrewed Theater is Tucson's premier volunteer-run nonprofit 501(c)(3) organization dedicated to the art of improv comedy. In the style of "Whose Line Is It Anyway?", performers create fast-paced scenes and games based entirely on audience suggestions and participation (Unscrewed Theater, Tripadvisor).
attractions.hours: Friday: 6:30 PM – 10:00 PM, Saturday: 6:00 PM – 10:00 PM, Sunday – Thursday: Closed (Tripadvisor)
attractions.admission: Adults: $10.00, Kids (under 12): $5.00, Live Stream: $10.00 per household (Unscrewed Theater, Tucson Stage)
highlights: Home to the "Not Burnt Out Just Unscrewed" improv troupe, performing since 2002 (Unscrewed Theater)
Family-friendly improv shows every Friday and Saturday night at 7:30 PM (Unscrewed Theater)
Uncensored "After Dark" shows for adults (17+) twice a month at 9:00 PM (Unscrewed Theater)
Dedicated training center offering improv classes and workshops for the community (Unscrewed Theater)
All live performances are available for streaming to audiences worldwide (Unscrewed Theater)
100% volunteer-run organization fostering a safe and supportive creative atmosphere (Unscrewed Theater)
attractions.tips: Save your ticket stub for discounts at neighboring restaurants like Dedicated Bakery and Mama’s Famous Pizza. The theater box office typically opens one hour before showtime, and while tickets are available at the door, online booking is recommended to secure a seat. For easy parking, turn at the Walgreens on Speedway and Swan to access the Midway Business Park lot (Unscrewed Theater, Tripadvisor).
attractions.nearby: Tucson Botanical Gardens, The Mini Time Machine Museum Of Miniatures, Reid Park Zoo, Trail Dust Town, Mama's Famous Pizza & Heros, Sushi Ten (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Wind Cave (Finger Rock Trail)
attractions.description: Wind Cave is a popular natural landmark and turnaround point located approximately two miles into the challenging Finger Rock Trail within the Pusch Ridge Wilderness of the Santa Catalina Mountains. It is characterized as a shallow rock alcove or overhang rather than a deep cavern, offering hikers a shaded resting spot with panoramic views of the canyon and the Tucson valley below PHOENIX magazine, Wander Woman.
attractions.region: Santa Catalina Mountains / Pusch Ridge Wilderness
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours (typically visited sunrise to sunset) PHOENIX magazine
attractions.admission: Free (Coronado National Forest / Pusch Ridge Wilderness) PHOENIX magazine, Wander Woman
attractions.phone: (520) 749-8700 (Santa Catalina Ranger District) U.S. Forest Service
highlights: Panoramic views of Finger Rock Canyon and the Tucson city skyline
Unique rock overhang providing natural shade and a scenic resting spot
Opportunities to spot local wildlife such as bighorn sheep and raptors
Lush desert vegetation including saguaros, ocotillos, and seasonal wildflowers
Proximity to the iconic Finger Rock pinnacle, a striking 4,000-foot stone formation
attractions.tips: The hike is considered difficult due to steep, rocky terrain and significant elevation gain; visitors should bring at least 2-3 liters of water and wear sturdy hiking boots. It is best to start early in the morning to avoid the intense Arizona heat, especially since much of the trail is exposed to the sun PHOENIX magazine, Wander Woman.
attractions.nearby: Finger Rock Spring, Mount Lemmon, Sabino Canyon Recreation Area, Ventana Canyon Trail PHOENIX magazine, Wander Woman

attractions.name: Xerocraft Hackerspace
attractions.description: Xerocraft is a member-supported makerspace and all-volunteer 501(c)3 nonprofit tool library cooperative in Tucson. It provides a shared workspace with a wide array of industrial tools, including 3D printers and welding equipment, where community members can learn, teach, and collaborate on diverse projects (Xerocraft About).
attractions.hours: Members: Monday-Sunday 8:00 AM - 10:00 PM; Public Tours: Saturday 1:00 PM - 3:00 PM (Shop Hours & Tours)
attractions.days_open: 365 days a year for members; Saturdays for public tours (Shop Hours & Tours)
attractions.admission: Standard: $65 (1 month non-recurring) or $60/month (recurring); Student: $30/month; Family: $95/month (2-3 persons); Volunteer: Based on hours; Tours: Free (Xerocraft Membership, Shop Hours & Tours)
highlights: Fully equipped Wood Shop and Metal Shop
3D Printing Lab and Laser Cutter/Engraver
Electronics Department and Machine Shop
Jewelry & Fused Glass Studio
Welding Shop and CNC Shop
attractions.tips: Guests can drop in for shop tours every Saturday afternoon without an appointment. To use the equipment, you must be a member and receive safety certification for the specific shop you wish to use. Note that visitors under 18 must be accompanied by a guardian at all times (Shop Hours & Tours).
